ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., router, switch, server) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the device was installed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., active, maintenance, decommissioned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  "max_temperature" REAL /* Maximum temperature the device can handle */,
  "min_temperature" REAL /* Minimum temperature the device can handle */,
  "max_network_traffic" REAL /* Maximum network traffic the device can handle */,
  "min_network_traffic" REAL /* Minimum network traffic the device can handle */,
  "maintenance_frequency" TEXT /* Recommended maintenance frequency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., temperature, network traffic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., Celsius, bytes) */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data (e.g., integer, float, string) */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the metric is critical */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the metric is collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reading */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the reading belongs to */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric the reading belongs to */,
  "reading_time" TEXT /* Time the reading was taken */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the reading */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the reading (e.g., normal, warning, critical) */,
  "device_location" TEXT /* Location of the device at the time of the reading */,
  "device_status" TEXT /* Status of the device at the time of the reading */,
  "anomaly_score" REAL /* Score indicating the likelihood of the reading being an anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_readings_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric the threshold belongs to */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Value of the threshold */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "max_critical_duration" INTEGER /* Maximum duration (in minutes) the metric can exceed the critical threshold before an alert is triggered */,
  "max_warning_duration" INTEGER /* Maximum duration (in minutes) the metric can exceed the warning threshold before an alert is triggered */,
  "notification_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which notifications should be sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thresholds_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* ID of the threshold that was breached */,
  "notification_time" TEXT /* Time the notification was sent */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message sent in the notification */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, acknowledged, dismissed) */,
  "acknowledged_time" TEXT /* Time the notification was acknowledged */,
  "acknowledged_by" TEXT /* User who acknowledged the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_threshold_id FOREIGN KEY ("threshold_id") REFERENCES thresholds ("threshold_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "is_admin" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user has administrative privileges */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object or array of permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time the user accessed the platform */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., login, view device) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the platform */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used to access the platform */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the access attempt (e.g., success, failure) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Additional description or context for the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
